text:
You are working on beads issue str-9ab: "Restructure orchestrator::explore() to separate observation from solving"

## Issue Details
Break the tight concolic loop in orchestrator::explore() into separate Observe and Solve/Generate phases. Observe: execute from all input sources, collect results. Solve/Generate: extract constraints from results, call Z3 for uncovered branches, produce new candidate inputs. The outer loop iterates: Observe round → Solve/Generate → feed new candidates → next Observe round. This creates the seam for the future pluggable generator trait. Define a SolveOutput or GenerateOutput type for what the solve phase produces.

Depends on str-1fl (rename ExplorationResult → ObservationOutput - done).

NOTE: str-jxs0 (Extract Z3Solver strategy) was just merged to main. Your worktree will be based on the latest main which includes that work. The Z3 solving logic is now also available as Z3SolverStrategy in strategy.rs, but the orchestrator still has the inline version. Your job is to restructure the orchestrator loop itself.

## WORKTREE SETUP — RUN FIRST
Before doing anything else, create and enter an isolated worktree:
```bash
REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
BRANCH_NAME="str-9ab"
WORKTREE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/$BRANCH_NAME"

if [ -d "$WORKTREE_DIR" ]; then
  echo "Worktree already exists, reusing"
else
  git worktree add "$WORKTREE_DIR" -b "$BRANCH_NAME"
fi

cd "$WORKTREE_DIR"
```
Verify you are in the right place:
```bash
echo "CWD: $(pwd)"
echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)"
echo "$(pwd)" | grep -q '\.claude/worktrees/' && echo "WORKTREE VERIFIED" || echo "FATAL: NOT in a worktree"
```
If verification fails, STOP and report the error to the team lead.

ALL subsequent commands must run from this worktree directory.
NEVER cd back to the main repo directory. Commit and push ONLY on your worktree branch — never touch main.

## Instructions
1. Follow CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md conventions
2. Read orchestrator.rs thoroughly to understand the current concolic loop
3. Define SolveOutput/GenerateOutput types for the solve phase output
4. Restructure the loop into Observe → Solve/Generate phases
5. Ensure E2E behavior is preserved (same branches discovered)
6. Add unit tests for the new phase separation
7. Run quality gates: `cargo test` and `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` in shatter-core
8. Run `/pre-completion` before reporting done — include its output in your completion message
9. Push your branch: `git push -u origin HEAD`

When done, mark task #9 as completed via TaskUpdate and send a message to the team lead.

{"type":"permission_response","request_id":"perm-1772942010246-cv2sacf","subtype":"success","response":{"updated_input":{"command":"git add shatter-core/src/orchestrator.rs && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: restructure orchestrator::explore() into Observe → Solve/Generate phases (str-9ab)

Separate the tight concolic loop into two distinct phases per iteration:
- Observe: execute one worklist entry, classify path (observe_one)
- Solve/Generate: extract constraints, run Z3/fuzz/drill (solve_and_generate)

New types: Observation, SolveOutput, ObserveOneResult, ExploreBudget.
This creates a clean seam for future pluggable generator traits while
preserving exact behavioral parity with the original interleaved loop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git status","description":"Stage, commit, and verify"},"permission_updates":[]}}
